finding: SetDepth stores the incoming depth into m_Depth at field offset 0x18 and then branches to ResetRenderDepth.
evidence: Observed `str w19, [x20, #0x18]` followed by branch to RVA 0x13408DC in Capstone output.
patch_implication: A runtime-equivalent reconstruction must not set only sibling order; it must call/equivalent SetDepth so m_Depth and render-depth reset both run.

finding: ResetRenderDepth lazily resolves m_UIFormBase at 0x20 and m_Canvas at 0x28, reads m_Depth at 0x18, reads the parent UIFormBase/CurrCanvas sorting order, then sets the helper Canvas sorting order to parent order plus m_Depth. It also iterates child render components and applies the combined depth.
evidence: Observed accesses to [this+0x20], [this+0x28], [this+0x18], addition of parent sorting value and m_Depth, and repeated calls over child component list.
patch_implication: UI_Dock source-built sibling placement is insufficient; nested helper canvases must receive parent form sorting order + local helper depth.

finding: ResetDepth mirrors the ResetRenderDepth path but uses the explicit depth argument, combines it with the parent UIFormBase/CurrCanvas order, then applies it to the helper Canvas and child render components.
evidence: Observed argument preserved in w19, parent canvas sorting read, `add w1, w21, w19`, and child iteration using the combined value.
patch_implication: OnDepthChanged/runtime depth updates must be propagated through CanvasHelper rather than approximated with hierarchy order.

finding: OnDepthChanged dispatches back into CanvasHelper depth-reset behavior. The direct path reaches ResetRenderDepth; shared/generic path may route through a runtime invoke helper.
evidence: Observed branch to ResetRenderDepth RVA 0x13408DC in the non-shared path.
patch_implication: The form Depth event is part of production rendering behavior and cannot be ignored in a candidate patch.

finding: YouYouUIManager.SetSortingOrder delegates to UILayer.SetSortingOrder through its UI layer field.
evidence: Observed load from manager field offset 0x20 followed by branch to UILayer.SetSortingOrder RVA 0xDCAC7C.
patch_implication: Candidate reconstruction needs the UI layer/group state, not only individual Canvas.sortingOrder constants.

finding: UILayer.SetSortingOrder uses UIFormBase.GroupId at offset 0x38 and layer dictionaries. In add/open path it increments the group sorting cursor by 0x64 (100) and applies the resulting value to UIFormBase.CurrCanvas at offset 0x40.
evidence: Observed GroupId load from [form+0x38], dictionary calls, `add w2, w0, #0x64`, and final Canvas sorting-order setter on CurrCanvas.
patch_implication: UI_Dock/UI_MainPage order depends on group cursor state and open order. Static sibling order alone cannot prove production order.

finding: UIFormBase.Init stores UIFormId, SysUIForm, CurrCanvas, CurrCanvasGroup, flags, user data, and initial enum state into known field offsets.
evidence: Observed stores matching dump.cs offsets including GroupId 0x38, CurrCanvas 0x40, CurrCanvasGroup 0x48, Depth 0x58, UserData 0x68.
patch_implication: Recovered field mapping makes later runtime dump/reconstruction checks more precise.

finding: UIFormBase.Open activates CurrCanvas, calls YouYouUIManager.SetSortingOrder(form, true) when the form is not DisableUILayer, reads CurrCanvas.sortingOrder, stores it into Depth at offset 0x58, and invokes OnDepthChanged at offset 0xA8.
evidence: Observed CurrCanvas access [form+0x40], DisableUILayer check [form+0x54], call to YouYouUIManager.SetSortingOrder RVA 0xFE1BA4 with add flag, Canvas sorting read, store to [form+0x58], and delegate invoke on [form+0xA8].
patch_implication: This explains why old-root + static UI_Dock sibling differed from production: production computes form Depth during Open and notifies CanvasHelpers.

finding: UISpineCtr.Awake gets SkeletonGraphic from the same GameObject and stores it in the private skeletonGraphic field at offset 0x18.
evidence: Observed GetComponent-like call and `str x0, [x19, #0x18]`; dump.cs field skeletonGraphic is at 0x18.
patch_implication: UI_Dock sp_* nodes need an actual SkeletonGraphic component. RectTransform-only placeholders cannot satisfy production behavior.

finding: PlayAnimation lazily ensures skeletonGraphic exists, clears/replaces prior Lua callback fields, and calls through SkeletonGraphic animation state APIs with track index, animation name, loop flag, and Lua callbacks.
evidence: Observed skeletonGraphic field [this+0x18], callback fields [0x20/0x28/0x30/0x38/0x40], null-clearing stores, and animation-state calls.
patch_implication: A source-backed renderer reconstruction must bind real SkeletonGraphic/SkeletonDataAsset/material, then allow UISpineCtr.PlayAnimation to drive it.

finding: PlayAnimation2 follows the same skeletonGraphic lazy-bind path and uses Action<string> callback storage instead of LuaFunction callbacks.
evidence: Observed skeletonGraphic field [this+0x18], Action field path, and animation-state calls.
patch_implication: This supports the same no-fake renderer boundary for UI_Dock sp_* animations.
